basics.name: Iman Bayat Makoo
basics.headline: Senior Product Manager
basics.tagline: Liquidity, exchange automation, and the metrics that prove impact
summary: Product Manager with 4+ years across fintech and consumer platforms, now specializing in crypto exchange and market-making at Tabdeal. I lead a squad of 5+ backend engineers and partner directly with C-suite and shareholders to improve liquidity quality, automate exchange operations, and contain financial risk.
I lifted market-maker uptime 15% by re-architecting liquidity around VWAP-based quality depth, and I engineer the proprietary metrics (Python, SQL, Metabase) that prove impact.
basics.availability: Want to talk markets, market making, or product? Reach out.

experience.title: Senior Product Manager — Market Maker Squad Lead

sections.heading: Revenue, Pricing & Growth
bullets: Owned end-to-end development of a proprietary market-making algorithm — from hypothesis through quantitative modeling, PRD, and cross-functional alignment across engineering, quant, and executive stakeholders — delivering a 60% revenue uplift validated in both backtesting and forward production tests.

sections.heading: Algorithmic Product & Quantitative Modeling
bullets: Lifted market-maker uptime by 15% by leading the build of exchange-wide safety tooling: a price-source kill-switch, automated price-gap alerting, and futures-based hedging for grid bots.
Modeled 80% co-delisting correlation between crypto and stablecoin pairs across major venues, converting the finding into a 30-day protective buffer policy that pre-empts cascading delisting risk.
Audited OTC transaction data and discovered 80% of retail swap volume settled below a key threshold; designed a tiered fee-and-slippage framework that captured the high-velocity revenue band.
Built an algorithmic depth-clustering framework that scores order-book depth against top competitors and auto-alerts when our depth skew drops below 20%.
Designed volatility-adaptive spread logic in the valuation engine so spreads scale to live market conditions without manual overrides.

sections.heading: Data-Driven Innovation, Automation, Tooling & AI
bullets: Delist Efficiency Score (DES): engineered a proprietary scoring system using Python-based data mining to quantify the operational performance of the asset delisting lifecycle.

Owned the master PRD and cross-functional dependencies for an end-to-end delist automation engine, eliminating redundant manual workflows and systemic risks across the delisting and listing pipeline — leading to a 30% decrease in users' delist complaints.
Prototyped LLM integration into quantitative workflows, using customized AI agents to accelerate global asset surveillance and competitor monitoring.
Built a Python surveillance bot scraping global exchange announcements, cutting the squad's response time to international delisting events from 3 hr on average to 15 min.
Engineered a Python ETL pipeline that parses, consolidates, and pushes user activation and trade data into central reporting — removing recurring manual reporting work for leadership.

sections.heading: Crisis Response & Risk Management
bullets: Led the postmortem of a major venue asset-freeze incident, securing full corporate absorption of the exposure so retail users took zero liquidation loss.
Convened a cross-CTO alignment summit with infrastructure leads to resolve root causes behind systemic failures, standardizing cross-departmental accountability for future incidents.

sections.heading: Squad Leadership & Stakeholder Management
bullets: Recruited and onboarded an Associate Product Manager; authored the technical curriculum covering P&L logic, matching-engine mechanics, and cohort vs. rolling-window retention analysis.
Delegated end-to-end product ownership of the automated Grid Trading Bot and Inventory-Based Price Skewing to an APM as a structured succession milestone.
Established the squad knowledgebase, standardizing and automating shift reporting, operational checklists, and knowledge-transfer protocols.

metrics.label: Response time to intl. delisting events

experience.title: Product Manager
bullets: Owned product strategy, pricing logic, and user-growth modeling for three B2B/IoT products: Connected Car, Heavy Fleet Management, and Cloud Surveillance.
Authored commercialization documents, operating-process maps, and revenue forecasts; drove cross-functional delivery with software and hardware-build teams.
Conducted domestic and international market studies to inform positioning and GTM.

experience.title: Product Marketing Manager
bullets: Ran competitive analysis and user segmentation that shaped acquisition targeting and product-marketing positioning for an early-stage Iranian crypto exchange.

experience.title: Product Manager
bullets: Owned end-to-end product for the LOEMS platform, including feature research, business-model design, and go-to-market positioning for an institutional execution tool.

experience.title: Key Account Manager
bullets: Managed key international developer accounts at Iran's largest Android marketplace.

experience.title: Developers Support — SearchAds Specialist
bullets: Built a semi-RFM segmentation of developer accounts and used it to upsell SearchAds revenue by 250%.

items.name: Delist Monitor & Forwarding
items.blurb: AI-assisted rapid prototyping of a Python alert system for international delisting events, giving the MM squad real-time intelligence on asset removals.

items.name: Operational Checklist Automation
items.blurb: Automated tracking of the 30+ steps of asset delisting with real-time timestamping to ensure 100% compliance.

items.name: Wallet UI Transformation
items.blurb: Dynamic "pinned asset" feature for user wallets during delisting events, significantly reducing customer friction and support tickets.
